title: Fermented Kenyan Cabbage Ssam
description: A flavorful, fermented dish featuring cabbage leaves, inspired by traditional Kenyan cuisine, perfect for a quick lunch or dinner, and suitable for Jain diets.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together coconut water, rice vinegar, salt, turmeric, black pepper, and ginger to create the fermentation liquid.
Add the shredded cabbage to the bowl and massage the liquid into the cabbage until it's evenly coated.
Cover the bowl with a lid or plastic wrap and let it ferment at room temperature (around 75°F) for 3-5 days, or until slightly tangy and slightly fermented.
Once fermented, transfer the cabbage to an airtight container or jar, leaving some space for expansion.
Store the fermented cabbage in the refrigerator to slow down the fermentation process.
When ready to serve, lay a ssam wrap flat and add a few leaves of the fermented cabbage.
Fold the bottom edge of the wrap up over the cabbage, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap up tightly to form a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining cabbage and wraps.
Serve the fermented cabbage ssam with your favorite accompaniments, such as kimchi or pickled carrots.
Enjoy your delicious, fermented Kenyan cabbage ssam!
